event.event_summary: Real-curated Apple Q2 2024 earnings replay with timestamped citations, replay-locked evidence, and held-out validation. Research and education only; not investment advice.

contradicting_evidence.title: Apple Inc. 10-Q filed 2024-02-02

expected_observables: Capital return should be prominent in near-term investor discussion.
Per-share support should matter more than reported product revenue weakness if the buyback narrative dominates.
Follow-up filings should preserve evidence of the newly authorized repurchase program.
narratives.mechanism: A larger repurchase authorization and higher dividend can change the near-term equity story from reported revenue softness to capital-return support for per-share value.
narratives.narrative: Investors focused on Apple's capital return reset after the company disclosed a new authorization to repurchase up to $110 billion of common stock and raised the quarterly dividend.

supporting_evidence.claim: MacRumors reported shortly after Apple's Q2 2024 release that Services set an all-time quarterly record, iPhone revenue fell by more than $5 billion year over year, and Apple authorized an additional $110 billion for share repurchases.
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: MacRumors reported shortly after Apple's Q2 2024 release that Services set an all-time quarterly record, iPhone revenue fell by more than $5 billion year over year, and Apple authorized an additional $110 billion for share repurchases.
supporting_evidence.document_text: MacRumors published a May 2, 2024 1:38 PM PDT report on Apple fiscal Q2 2024. The report summarized revenue of $90.8 billion and profit of $23.6 billion, said Services set an all-time quarterly record at $23.9 billion, noted iPhone revenue dropped by more than $5 billion versus the year-ago quarter, and noted Apple authorized an additional $110 billion for share repurchases.
supporting_evidence.title: Apple Reports 2Q 2024 Results: $23.6B Profit on $90.8B Revenue

supporting_evidence.claim: Nasdaq / Business Wire published Apple's fiscal Q2 2024 results release at 4:30 PM EDT on May 2, 2024, including lower year-over-year revenue, an all-time Services revenue record, a March-quarter EPS record, a dividend increase, and authorization for up to $110 billion in additional share repurchases.
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Nasdaq / Business Wire published Apple's fiscal Q2 2024 results release at 4:30 PM EDT on May 2, 2024, including lower year-over-year revenue, an all-time Services revenue record, a March-quarter EPS record, a dividend increase, and authorization for up to $110 billion in additional share repurchases.
supporting_evidence.document_text: Nasdaq / Business Wire published Apple's fiscal Q2 2024 results release at 4:30 PM EDT on May 2, 2024. The release reported quarterly revenue of $90.8 billion, down 4 percent year over year; Services revenue at a new all-time record; March-quarter diluted EPS of $1.53; a higher quarterly dividend; and a new authorization to repurchase up to $110 billion of common stock.
supporting_evidence.title: Apple reports second quarter results

narratives.title: Capital return reset

expected_observables: Services should remain a central offset to hardware category declines.
Gross margin resilience should matter in interpretation of the quarter.
Future discussion should compare Services growth with product revenue softness.
narratives.mechanism: Services growth and mix can protect gross margin and earnings quality even when hardware categories decline.
narratives.narrative: The quarter was interpreted through Services resilience: Services revenue grew while several product categories declined, giving investors a higher-margin offset to weaker hardware demand.

narratives.title: Services mix resilience

expected_observables: Hardware-focused analysis should emphasize iPhone, iPad, and Wearables declines.
If this narrative dominates, later commentary should focus on unit demand or product-cycle weakness.
Capital return and Services growth would need to be treated as offsets rather than primary drivers.
narratives.mechanism: Broad product-category declines can signal weaker hardware demand and pressure the durability of future revenue growth.
narratives.narrative: The event exposed hardware demand weakness: iPhone, iPad, and Wearables revenue declined year over year while total net sales fell.

narratives.title: Hardware demand pressure

expected_observables: Greater China should remain a named risk if the regional narrative is correct.
Later filings or commentary should show whether China weakness persists or stabilizes.
The explanation should be weaker if non-China regions and Services carry the investment debate.
narratives.mechanism: Weakness in Greater China can create a region-specific demand concern that investors may separate from company-wide category mix.
narratives.narrative: Investors were weighing regional pressure, especially Greater China, where reported net sales declined and iPhone represented a larger proportion of segment sales.

supporting_evidence.claim: Segment Operating Performance The following table shows net sales by reportable segment for the three months ended December 30, 2023 and December 31, 2022 (dollars in millions): Three Months Ended December 30, 2023 December 31, 2022 Change Net sales by reportable segment: Americas $ 50,430 $ 49,278 2 % Europe 30,397 27,681 10 % Greater China 20,819 23,905 (13) % Japan 7,767 6,755 15 % Rest of Asia Pacific 10,162 9,535 7 % Total net sales $ 119,575 $ 117,154 2 % Americas Americas net sales increased 2% or $1.2 billion during the first quarter of 2024 compared to the same quarter in 2023 due primarily to higher net sales of Services and iPhone, partially offset by lower net sales of iPad. The strength in foreign currencies relative to the U.S. dollar had a net favorable year-over-year impact on Americas net sales during the first quarter of 2024.
